skill.name: using-git-worktrees
skill.description: Creates isolated git worktrees for feature development with smart directory selection and safety verification
skill.summary: Set up isolated workspaces for parallel branch work without switching contexts
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 43 static findings evaluated as false positives. External commands are standard git and build tools with hardcoded or trusted arguments. Filesystem operations are legitimate directory checks. No command injection vectors, credential exposure, or malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Create isolated git workspaces for feature development
content.value_statement: Developers struggle with context switching when working on multiple branches. This skill creates isolated git worktrees so you can work on features in parallel without losing your current workspace state.
actual_capabilities: Detects existing worktree directories and follows priority order for selection
Verifies worktree directories are git-ignored before creation
Creates new git worktrees with automatic branch creation
Auto-detects project type and runs appropriate setup commands
Runs baseline tests to verify clean workspace state
Reports worktree location and test results upon completion
limitations: Requires git repository with worktree support enabled
Cannot create worktrees on branches with uncommitted changes in the main working directory
Project setup commands limited to npm, cargo, pip, poetry, and go mod
Requires user input when no worktree directory preference is configured

use_cases.title: Feature Development Isolation
use_cases.description: Start work on a new feature branch without losing uncommitted changes or breaking the current workspace state
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developer working on multiple features simultaneously

use_cases.title: Code Review Preparation
use_cases.description: Create a clean isolated workspace to review and test pull request changes without affecting local development
use_cases.target_user: Senior developer conducting thorough code reviews

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Reproduction
use_cases.description: Isolate bug investigation work in a separate worktree while maintaining stable development environment

prompt_templates.title: Basic Worktree Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up an isolated workspace for the feature/auth-improvements branch using git worktrees
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new authentication feature with a clean workspace

prompt_templates.title: Worktree with Custom Directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a worktree for bugfix/memory-leak in the .worktrees directory and run the project setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating worktree in a specific location with automatic dependency installation

prompt_templates.title: PR Review Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an isolated worktree from the pull-request-123 branch so I can review changes without affecting my current work
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a clean environment for code review and testing

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Branch Development
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to work on the experimental/caching branch while keeping my current changes intact. Set up a worktree and verify tests pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Parallel development requiring workspace isolation and baseline verification

output_examples.input: Create a worktree for the new payment feature
output_examples.output:
I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace.

Worktree ready at /projects/myapp/.worktrees/feature/payments
Tests passing (47 tests, 0 failures)
Ready to implement payment feature

output_examples.input: Set up a workspace to review the api-v2 branch
output_examples.output:
I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace.

Detected existing .worktrees directory
Verified directory is git-ignored
Created worktree: git worktree add .worktrees/api-v2 -b api-v2
Installed dependencies: npm install
Baseline tests: 23 passing

Worktree ready at /projects/myapp/.worktrees/api-v2

best_practices: Always verify worktree directories are git-ignored before creation to prevent accidental commits
Run baseline tests immediately after worktree creation to establish a clean starting point
Follow the directory priority order: existing directory > CLAUDE.md preference > ask user
anti_patterns: Creating worktrees without verifying git ignore status, causing workspace pollution in version control
Skipping baseline test verification, making it impossible to distinguish new bugs from pre-existing issues
Hardcoding directory locations instead of checking existing directories and user preferences first

faq.question: What is a git worktree and why use it?
faq.answer: A git worktree is a linked working directory that shares the same repository. It allows you to work on multiple branches simultaneously without switching, keeping each workspace isolated and preserving uncommitted changes.

faq.question: Where are worktrees created?
faq.answer: Worktrees are created in either .worktrees/ (project-local), worktrees/ (alternative project-local), or ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/<project>/ (global location). The skill follows a priority order and asks if no preference exists.

faq.question: Do I need to install dependencies in each worktree?
faq.answer: Yes, each worktree has its own working directory. The skill automatically detects your project type (npm, cargo, pip, poetry, go) and runs the appropriate install command.

faq.question: What happens if baseline tests fail?
faq.answer: The skill reports the test failures and asks whether you want to proceed with investigation or continue anyway. This ensures you know the starting state before making new changes.

faq.question: Can I have multiple worktrees at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, git supports multiple worktrees. Each worktree operates independently, allowing you to work on different branches in parallel with separate terminal sessions.

faq.question: How do I clean up worktrees after finishing?
faq.answer: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill or run 'git worktree remove <path>' to delete a worktree. The main repository and other worktrees remain unaffected.
